commit_message:

I remind that the rule set for merging into OOM is the following:
- If you're component is a core component (AAI, DMAAP MR, SDC, SDNC, SO, VID), you must have 100% Healthchecks and end2end tests that are passing
- for the others, one test can fail for healthchecks and one for e2e

For now, we were able to follow this rule and merge all the patches (the one that weren't good were because of issue in the component).
Unfortunately, after 37 attempts (many of them being done when gate was broken) on this patch, we were not able to be successful.

For information, the 2 "children" patches (so patch with this change + more update on SO components) were able to met this goal this week end.

I've then taken a look on the last 12 attempts:
* 4 were not good because of gate problem (500 error from ansible galaxy, I'm working to mitigate this)
* 8 were not successful on at least one the 5 e2e use cases (basic_vm -- alacarte, basic_network -- alacarte, basic_cnf -- alacarte, PNF Registrate -- Flow mode, 5GBulkPM -- no idea)

On these 8 we had:
* 5 successful basic_vm
* 5 successful basic_network
* 3 successful basic_cnf
* 4 successful PNF registrate
* 7 successful 5GBulkPM

errors are viewed in the part of the following components:
* SDC: 6 times
* SO: 7 times
* AAI: 1 time (PNF registrate)
* Unknown (no logs): 2 times (one time PNF registrate, one time 5GBulkPM)

Even if we didn't manage to have a successful 100% e2e tests in these 12 tests, I recommend to merge this patch.
Although, I believe it's critical to make SDC and SO more reliable.

Unless for bugfixes for Honolulu, I then propose to only merge patches from SO and SDNC that significantly improves the gating results (My goal is to have 1 error per 5 gates instead of ~1 per gate).
